title: fix sphinx build to include podman-create/run page
body:
When docs.podman.io is build on readthedocs we have to make sure to
generate the markdown pages first.

It works locally with sphinx but I have no idea if this works on the
readthedocs infra.

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
None
```

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *Luap99* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [Luap99] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

LGTM, although I must confess I don't know anything about `conf.py` or what it's used for. I'm happy to merge and see what happens.

I will likely remove the `remove{prefix,suffix}` additions in a future update, replacing with a specific `podman_subcommand()` function instead of general-purpose ones, but this is fine as it is. Thank you for catching that and doing the work.

/lgtm
I only checked it locally as described here: https://github.com/containers/podman/tree/main/docs#local-testing
Confirmed, the [latest build](https://readthedocs.org/projects/podman/builds/17623811/) worked successfully and https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-create.1.html is visible again.
